channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.113
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.113 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Power Apps Vibe Experience: Build Business Apps at the Speed of Ideas
item.description: Power Apps Vibe Experience: Building Business Applications with AI in Minutes Organizations today operate in a fast-paced digital environment where new business challenges emerge constantly. Whether it’s managing internal workflows, tracking projects, or collecting customer feedback, businesses...
item.dc:creator: harshul05

item.title: Centralized Monitoring in Azure
item.description: Centralized Monitoring in Azure: Automating Diagnostic Settings Across All Resources Introduction In modern cloud environments, observability is not optional—it’s foundational. As organizations scale their Azure footprint, ensuring consistent monitoring across hundreds (or thousands) of resources...
item.dc:creator: Shikhaghildiyal

item.title: Heroku Set the Standard. We're Just Removing the Ceiling
item.description: For a generation of developers, Heroku was where it clicked — the moment you realized shipping software didn't have to mean fighting infrastructure. Push your code, get a URL, focus on your product. That idea changed an industry, and a lot of us built our first real apps on it. Now that Heroku has...
item.dc:creator: simonjj

item.title: More visibility into Copilot coding agent sessions
item.description: When you delegate a task to Copilot coding agent, it works in the background, then requests your review. You can view the agent session logs to understand what Copilot did… The post More visibility into Copilot coding agent sessions appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Accelerating .NET MAUI Development with AI Agents
item.description: Learn how custom-built AI agents are dramatically improving the .NET MAUI contribution workflow, reducing issue resolution time by 50-70% while increasing test coverage and code quality. The post Accelerating .NET MAUI Development with AI Agents appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Ortinau

item.title: GitHub Actions: Late March 2026 updates
item.description: This month, GitHub Actions resolves some outstanding papercuts, including support for timezones in scheduled workflows and using environments without automatic deployments. GitHub Actions now allows developers to use environments without… The post GitHub Actions: Late March 2026 updates appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing Azure Managed Grafana MCP: The Managed Telemetry Gateway for AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are rapidly becoming a core part of how teams build, operate, and improve cloud systems, from coding assistants to autonomous remediation workflows. To deliver on that promise in the enterprise, agents need a secure, governed way to access real production telemetry. Azure Managed Grafana...
item.dc:creator: aayodeji

item.title: Building an Enterprise Platform for Inference at Scale
item.description: Architecture Decisions With the optimization stack in place, the next layer of decisions is architectural — how you distribute compute across GPUs, nodes, and deployment environments to match your model size and traffic profile. GPU Parallelism Strategy on AKS Strategy How It Works When to Use...
item.dc:creator: bobmital

item.title: Actions Runner Controller release 0.14.0
item.description: GitHub Actions Runner Controller (ARC) 0.14.0 is now generally available. This release introduces multilabel support for runner scale sets, switches to the actions/scaleset library client, adds resource customization options, and… The post Actions Runner Controller release 0.14.0 appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster
item.description: We’ve optimized Copilot coding agent so it now starts work 50% faster. You can hand work to Copilot coding agent in many ways, including assigning an issue to Copilot, entering… The post Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: New tools and guidance: Announcing Zero Trust for AI
item.description: Microsoft introduces Zero Trust for AI, adding a new AI pillar to its workshop, enhanced reference architecture, updated guidance, and a new assessment tool. The post New tools and guidance: Announcing Zero Trust for AI appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Mike Adams

item.title: Rethinking open source mentorship in the AI era
item.description: As contribution volume grows, mentorship signals are harder to read. The 3 Cs framework helps maintainers mentor more strategically... without burning out. The post Rethinking open source mentorship in the AI era appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cabunoc Mayes

item.title: Remote MCP Server preview in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Earlier this week we release the public preview for our Azure DevOps MCP Server. Today we are excited to let you know that the Azure DevOps MCP Server is now available to use in Microsoft Foundry. For those who are new to Foundry, Microsoft Foundry is a unified platform for building and managing AI...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Codespaces with data residency now available in Japan
item.description: GitHub Codespaces with data residency now supports Japan as an available region, in addition to the existing EU and Australia regions. In January, we announced that Codespaces is now in… The post Codespaces with data residency now available in Japan appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Azure Front Door: Resiliency Series – Part 2: Faster recovery (RTO)
item.description: In Part 1 of this blog series, we outlined our four‑pillar strategy for resiliency in Azure Front Door: configuration resiliency, data plane resiliency, tenant isolation, and accelerated Recovery Time Objective (RTO). Together, these pillars help Azure Front Door remain continuously available and...
item.dc:creator: AbhishekTiwari

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks OneLake Catalog Federation
item.description: You can now enable OneLake catalog federation in Azure Databricks to query Microsoft Fabric OneLake data without copying or moving it. By connecting Unity Catalog to a OneLake catalog, Azure Databricks automatically syncs schemas and tables so Fabric Lake
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Migrating to the next generation of Virtual Nodes on Azure Container Instances (ACI)
item.description: What is ACI/Virtual Nodes? Azure Container Instances (ACI) is a fully-managed serverless container platform which gives you the ability to run containers on-demand without provisioning infrastructure. Virtual Nodes on ACI allows you to run Kubernetes pods managed by an AKS cluster in a serverless...
item.dc:creator: adamsharif-msft

item.title: Retirement: HC-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description: On May 31, 2027, Microsoft Azure will retire the following HC-series virtual machine (VMs) sizes: Standard_HC44rs, Standard_HC44-16rs, and Standard_HC44-32rs. To ensure continuity and improved performance, we recommend transitioning to one of the current‑
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: HBv2-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description: On May 31, 2027, Microsoft Azure will retire the following HBv2-series virtual machine (VMs) sizes: Standard_HB120rs_v2, Standard_HB120-96rs_v2, Standard_HB120-64rs_v2, Standard_HB120-32rs_v2, and Standard_HB120-16rs_v2. To ensure continuity and improved
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: NP-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description: On May 31, 2027, Microsoft Azure will retire the follow NP-series virtual machine (VMs) sizes: Standard_NP10s, Standard_NP20s, and Standard_NP40s. To ensure continuity and optimal performance, we recommend transitioning to one of the following Azure GPU V
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page
item.description: Docked panels for the pull request “Files changed” page are rolling out now. They let you review code with key pull request context open side-by-side: overview, comments, merge status, and… The post View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available
item.description: Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available. Starting today, hierarchy view is enabled by default for all new project views. If you have existing views, you can turn… The post Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: How Squad runs coordinated AI agents inside your repository
item.description: An inside look at repository-native orchestration with GitHub Copilot and the design patterns behind multi-agent workflows that stay inspectable, predictable, and collaborative. The post How Squad runs coordinated AI agents inside your repository appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Brady Gaster

item.title: When tax season becomes cyberattack season: Phishing and malware campaigns using tax-related lures
item.description: During tax season, threat actors reliably take advantage of the urgency and familiarity of time-sensitive emails, including refund notices, payroll forms, filing reminders, and requests from tax professionals, to push malicious attachments, links, or QR codes. The post When tax season becomes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Azure Bastion: Enterprise-grade secure access made simple
item.description: Managing secure remote access to virtual machines traditionally means juggling public IP addresses, configuring jump boxes, deploying VPN infrastructure, and managing complex firewall rules. Each layer adds cost, complexity, and potential security vulnerabilities. Azure Bastion changes everything....
item.dc:creator: ShabazShaik

item.title: Building Knowledge-Grounded AI Agents with Foundry IQ
item.description: Foundry IQ now integrates with Foundry Agent Service via MCP (Model Context Protocol), enabling developers to build AI agents grounded in enterprise knowledge. This integration combines Foundry IQ’s intelligent retrieval capabilities with Foundry Agent Service’s orchestration, enabling agents to...
item.dc:creator: NelsonKumari

item.title: From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows
item.description: In this post we explore the evolution from Azure IoT Operations Data Processor Pipelines to Dataflows, why we adopted a hybrid strategy with custom Rust pods, and the architectural lessons we learned building event detection systems at the edge. The post From Azure IoT Operations Data Processor...
item.dc:creator: Maho Pacheco

item.title: ICYMI: Microsoft Agent 365 AMA
item.description: Watch the video of the live AMA to see experts from the Microsoft Agent 365 team answer questions from viewers. Recorded on March 18, 2026. Huge thanks to our community for contributing such great questions, plus thank you to our host and expert panelists for a great AMA! Still have questions?...
item.dc:creator: Nichole_Peterson

item.title: Introducing Secret Finder: Finding Real Credentials Where Traditional Tools Fail
item.description: Secret Finder is an AI-powered capability in Microsoft Security Copilot that detects leaked credentials in unstructured content, such as emails, chat logs, documents, and screenshots, where traditional pattern-matching tools struggle. It relies on a multi‑step, multi‑agent reasoning workflow rather...
item.dc:creator: Zixiao_Chen

item.title: Azure NCv6 Virtual Machines: Enhancements and GA Transition
item.description: NCv6 Virtual Machines are Azure's flexible, next generation platform enabling both leading-edge graphics and generative AI compute workloads. Featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (BSE) GPUs, Intel Xeon™ 6 "Granite Rapids" 6900P series CPUs, and a suite of Microsoft Azure...
item.dc:creator: Fernando_Aznar

item.title: Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools
item.description: When Copilot coding agent writes code, it automatically runs your project’s tests and linter. It also runs GitHub’s security and quality validation tools, including CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret… The post Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Authentication Tokens Are Not a Data Contract
item.description: Authentication tokens exist to answer one question: is this caller authorized to do this? They are not intended to be a stable data interface, a schema you can depend on, or an input into application logic. If your application decodes tokens and reads claims from them, this is an important...
item.dc:creator: Angel Wong

item.title: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description: Effective March 31, 2027, the Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure hosted by Power BI will be retired. After this date, the dashboard will no longer be accessible and technical support will be discontinued. We strongly encourage customers to export their
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing the New Home for the Azure Databricks Blog
item.description: We’re excited to share that the Azure Databricks blog has moved to a new address on Microsoft Tech Community Hub! Azure Databricks | Microsoft Community Hub Our new blog home is designed to make it easier than ever for you to discover the latest product updates, deep technical insights, and...
item.dc:creator: AnaviNahar

item.title: [Launched] General availability: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description: In mid-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Publish SQL database projects directly from Visual Studio Code to streamline database deployment workflows. View and edit SQL table data directly in the MSSQ
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Managed Identity and Workload Identity
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift now supports managed identities and workload identities as a generally available capability, enabling you to run OpenShift clusters and applications on Azure without long lived service principal credentials. This aligns Azure Red H
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public preview: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer, expanding the visual schema design experience with AI-assisted capabilities. Based on developer feedback and evolving workflows, you can now understan
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Database DevOps in SSMS powered by SQL projects
item.description: Bring the benefits of schema-as-code to your database development workflow. SQL database projects enable you to source control your database schema, perform reliable deployments to any environment, and integrate code quality checks into your development p
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description: Announcing SQL MCP Server, our Model Context Protocol (MCP) connector for production databases. SQL MCP Server is a feature rich component of data API builder (DAB) that gives you a simple, predictable, secure way to bring AI agents into your data workflo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22
item.description: GitHub Copilot is now generally available in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) 22, bringing AI‑powered assistance directly into the application that you use every day. With GitHub Copilot in SSMS, you can use natural language to develop, explain, fix,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: The Swarm Diaries: What Happens When You Let AI Agents Loose on a Codebase
item.description: The Idea Single-agent coding assistants are impressive, but they have a fundamental bottleneck: they think serially. Ask one to build a full CLI app with a database layer, a command parser, pretty output, and tests, and it’ll grind through each piece one by one. Industry benchmarks bear this out:...
item.dc:creator: larohra

item.title: Securing Production Debugging in Kubernetes
item.description: During production debugging, the fastest route is often broad access such as cluster-admin (a ClusterRole that grants administrator-level access), shared bastions/jump boxes, or long-lived SSH keys. It works in the moment, but it comes with two common problems: auditing becomes difficult, and...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd
item.description: New azd ai agent run and invoke commands let you start and test AI agents from your terminal—locally or in the cloud. The post Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan, Travis Angevine

item.title: v1.15.0-beta1
item.description: 1.15.0-beta1 (March 18, 2026) NEW FEATURES: We now produce builds for Windows ARM64 (#32719) You can set a deprecated attribute on variable and output blocks to indicate that they are deprecated. This will produce warnings when passing in a value for a deprecated variable or when referencing a...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: DiskANN Vector Index Improvements
item.description: Remember when we announced the Public Preview of DiskANN vector indexes back in November and mentioned that once you created the index, your table became read‑only? Yeah… about that… 😅 We shipped early because the demand for Vector search in SQL was overwhelming. We knew the constraints weren’t...
item.dc:creator: Davide Mauri, Pooja Kamath

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Lakeflow Connect Free Tier now available in Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now offers a Lakeflow Connect Free Tier that gives each workspace 100 free DBUs per workspace per day for ingestion from SaaS applications and databases. This allows you to bring roughly 100 million records of data per workspace per day i
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Versionless key support for transparent data encryption in Azure SQL Database
item.description: Azure SQL Database now supports versionless keys for transparent data encryption (TDE), giving you a simpler and more reliable way to manage your own encryption keys. You no longer need to reference a specific key version from Azure Key Vault or Managed H
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration, bringing a guided back-end generation experience directly into your development workflow. You can generate REST, GraphQL
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.112
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.112 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team
